strengths: 15+ years of professional full-stack experience with TypeScript and Node.js as a consistent core across Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq — directly matching the primary stack's most critical layers.
Current role at re:cinq includes explicit AI-agent infrastructure work and MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework development, precisely satisfying the spec's rare 'AI agent development' requirement.
Lifelong TDD practitioner who authored open-source testing frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts) and has taught TDD workshops — brings testing depth and culture well beyond checkbox familiarity.
concerns: Frontend experience centers on Ember.js rather than React; the conceptual model transfers but React proficiency is not evidenced and Nest.js is absent from the resume entirely.
Vitest is not mentioned; testing framework experience is demonstrated through D-language tooling and TDD practice rather than the specific Jest/Vitest ecosystem the team uses.
Kotlin is not present in the resume despite being listed as a secondary backend technology requirement.
response.justification: Bogdan is a rare candidate who simultaneously covers the role's hardest-to-hire requirement — AI agent and MCP framework development, demonstrated in his current position — and the everyday full-stack depth the team depends on, with TypeScript, Node.js, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, MongoDB, and MySQL all evidenced across production systems. His track record of rapidly adopting unfamiliar technologies (picking up iOS development mid-project, writing low-latency systems in DLang, contributing to embedded and robotics tooling) makes the React and Nest.js gap a learning-curve risk rather than a disqualifying absence, particularly for a team that could onboard him against a familiar Node/TypeScript base while he ramps on the framework specifics.
